title: Dan Dan Noodles Sichuan

seo.title: How to Make Dan Dan Noodles Sichuan — Easy Chinese Recipe

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Chinese Dan Dan Noodles Sichuan recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Dan Dan Noodles Sichuan — a beloved Chinese classic

description: Dan Dan Noodles Sichuan is a classic street food from Sichuan, China, known for its fiery, savory, and nutty flavor profile. This iconic dish features springy noodles tossed in a rich, spicy sauce, topped with a flavorful minced pork mixture and often garnished with preserved vegetables. Each bite offers a harmonious blend of chili oil, Sichuan peppercorns, sesame paste, and soy sauce, creating an unforgettable culinary experience.

ingredients.item: Chinese Egg Noodles or Wheat Noodles

ingredients.notes: per serving bowl, adjust to taste

ingredients.item: Chicken Broth or Noodle Cooking Water

instructions.title: Prepare the Pork Mixture
instructions.text: Marinate the ground pork with 1 teaspoon light soy sauce, 1 teaspoon Shaoxing wine, 1 teaspoon cornstarch, and 1/2 teaspoon sesame oil for 15 minutes. Heat 1 tablespoon of cooking oil in a wok or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated pork and stir-fry until it is cooked through and crispy, breaking it up with a spatula. Add the finely chopped preserved Sichuan mustard greens (Ya Cai) and continue to stir-fry for another 2-3 minutes until fragrant. Set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce Bases
instructions.text: In each individual serving bowl, combine 2-3 tablespoons chili oil, 1 tablespoon light soy sauce, 1 tablespoon Chinkiang vinegar, 1 tablespoon Chinese sesame paste, 1 teaspoon sugar, 1/2 teaspoon freshly ground Sichuan peppercorn powder, 1 minced garlic clove, and 1/2 teaspoon grated ginger if using. Stir well to form a smooth, emulsified sauce base at the bottom of each bowl.

instructions.title: Cook the Noodles
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Add the fresh or dried Chinese noodles and cook according to package directions until they are al dente. For fresh noodles, this usually takes 2-3 minutes. For dried noodles, it may take 5-7 minutes, ensuring they are cooked but still have a slight bite.

instructions.title: Loosen the Sauce
instructions.text: Before draining the noodles, carefully scoop 2-3 tablespoons of the hot noodle cooking water into each prepared sauce bowl. Stir vigorously with a spoon or chopsticks to thoroughly loosen and incorporate the sauce ingredients, creating a flavorful broth at the bottom of the bowl.

instructions.title: Combine and Serve
instructions.text: Drain the cooked noodles thoroughly and immediately transfer them into the prepared sauce bowls. Toss the noodles vigorously with the sauce using chopsticks or tongs until every strand is evenly coated with the rich, aromatic sauce. Ensure the sauce from the bottom is well distributed.

instructions.title: Garnish and Enjoy
instructions.text: Top each bowl of noodles with a generous spoonful of the stir-fried minced pork and preserved mustard greens mixture. Garnish with crushed roasted peanuts for texture and chopped green onions for freshness and color. Serve immediately while hot and savor the authentic flavors of Dan Dan Noodles Sichuan.

tips: For the most authentic flavor, use Chinese sesame paste, which is darker and more robust than Middle Eastern tahini. If using tahini, add a touch of sesame oil for depth.
Always use freshly ground Sichuan peppercorn powder. Toast whole Sichuan peppercorns lightly in a dry pan until fragrant, then grind them for maximum numbing and aromatic effect.
The key to a good Dan Dan Noodle sauce is balancing the five flavors: spicy, savory, sour, sweet, and numbing. Adjust chili oil, vinegar, and sugar to your preference.
article: Dan Dan Noodles Sichuan is a beloved Chinese Pasta and Noodles dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
